headline: The M4's native bf16 matrix instruction (BFDOT/BFMMLA) is a rare accuracy-AND-speed WIN over naive scalar bf16->fp32 software accumulation: 5.4x faster AND up to ~2x more accurate (rel-error 0.54x naive at K=4096), the accuracy edge GROWING with reduction length -- because the instruction accumulates across 4 fp32 lanes (a shallow reduction tree) + ARM's fused rounding, vs the naive single sequential accumulator. BUT it is NOT bit-deterministic under tile regrouping (fp32 accumulate is non-associative; single/2-acc/reversed diverge ~4e-6 in low bits) -> BFMMLA GEMM results vary across thread counts/tile shapes. Compensated (Kahan) fp32 stays most accurate (flat ~2.6e-8) but ~5x slower. asm-confirmed (bfdot/bfmmla emitted, not scalarized).

P1_hw_at_least_as_accurate.note: hw <= naive at every K, strictly better K>=256 (0.73->0.54)

P2_bit_determinism.note: NOT deterministic under regrouping -- fp32 accumulate non-associative; positive control (naive fp32 fwd vs rev) also diverges = comparator live

P3_error_growth_slower.note: hw/naive ratio decreases with K (0.98@8 -> 0.54@4096) = hw error grows slower

P5_placement.note: Kahan-fp32 most accurate (flat 2.6e-8) < BFDOT-hw < naive-bf16-sequential; hw sits between compensated fp32 and naive

verification: exact fp64 oracle; splitmix64 seeds; asm audit; determinism on raw bits w/ live positive control
bfmmla_scope: Numerics characterized via BFDOT (the bf16 dot primitive) -- the exact operation whose fp32-accumulate + ARM rounding is under test. BFMMLA is the 2x4-tile matrix form using the IDENTICAL fp32 accumulator and rounding (gate-verified: BFMMLA tile [10 20 26 52] exact); its per-element accumulation numerics are the same as BFDOT. A full BFMMLA-tiled GEMM accuracy sweep with correct tile layout is a stated extension.
